name: Discovering Quintandona — circular route
name_original: À descoberta de Quintandona — percurso circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lagares offer a diverse landscape characterized by varied terrain, from challenging ascents to more gentle paths along river valleys. The region features natural waterways, including river beaches, and historical sites. This combination provides a range of options for touring cyclists seeking both physical activity and scenic exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Lagares The most popular touring cycling route is From Quintandona to Santa Justa — circular route through the Regional Protected Landscape Park of the Porto Mountains, a 22.2 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores a protected regional park with varied topography. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo, a difficult 25.8 miles (41.5 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Ponte de Espindo – Statue of Saint James loop from Oleiros, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Lagares is defined by diverse landscapes including river valleys, historical points of interest, and the foothills of Serra da Estrela. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Lagares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 260 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lagares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lagares?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Lagares, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lagares offers some easier routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380631735/discovering-quintandona-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Discovering Quintandona — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.8 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride or families.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32331604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz Pergola – Arrábida Bridge loop from Terronhas&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 66.5 km path, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crasto – Menir de Luzim loop from Perozelo&lt;/a&gt;, a 48.9 km route with significant elevation gain, offer substantial distances and demanding terrain.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Lagares?
A: Yes, many routes around Lagares are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380651414/from-quintandona-to-santa-justa-circular-route-through-the-regional-protected-landscape-park-of-the-porto-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Quintandona to Santa Justa — circular route through the Regional Protected Landscape Park of the Porto Mountains&lt;/a&gt; and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380631735/discovering-quintandona-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Discovering Quintandona — circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in Lagares?
A: The region boasts diverse natural landscapes. You can expect to cycle through areas near the Serra da Estrela Nature Park, offering varied topography. Many routes also feature picturesque river valleys and access to river beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7269969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Choupal de Pedorido river beach&lt;/a&gt;, providing scenic stops.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area features notable water attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2863992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora do Salto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1113391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferreira River in the Couce Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which can be integrated into your cycling tours for beautiful natural sights.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit while cycling in Lagares?
A: Cyclists interested in history can explore sites such as the Roman Ruins of Bobadela, located just 5.2 km from Lagares. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo&lt;/a&gt; route offers a chance to visit the charming Quintandona Schist Village.

Q: Are there routes with good viewpoints or scenic spots?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several scenic spots. You can find excellent views at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5801557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doll's Swing&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3318098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspended Bridge at Parque das Serras do Porto&lt;/a&gt;, both offering memorable sights along your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Lagares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lagares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging ascents to picturesque river valleys, and the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lagares?
A: Lagares, with its diverse natural landscapes and river beaches, is generally enjoyable for touring cycling during warmer months when you can fully appreciate the natural waterways and outdoor scenery. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't detailed, the pleasant natural environment suggests spring through autumn would be ideal.

Q: Are there accommodation options near the cycling routes for multi-day tours?
A: Yes, for those planning multi-day cycling tours, facilities like Camping Toca da Raposa are conveniently located just 3.4 km from Lagares, providing a practical accommodation option for cyclists.
tips: The schist village of Quintadona is very well preserved. Its emblematic canastros are worth a visit.
